parsed_review.summary: CP00-897 opens RP30 People HR Evidence as an embedded HRX descriptor-only baseline (10 units, risk A, RP30.P00.M00.S01-RP30.P00.M05.S02, 8 implementation + 2 security_audit). The pack-level claim under review is hrx_contract_acceptance_baseline_descriptor_verified with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; that claim is fully supported. Evidence is internally consistent across the live plan, manifest, baseline, contract projection, registry/validators, and tests: unit/deliverable/micro-title distributions match HRX_CP897_REQUIREMENTS, all 15 mandatory artifacts exist, every referenced validation npm script (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/test/validate/spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane plus rp30/hrx ledger validators) is defined, and packages/hrx/test/*.test.js is included in `npm test`. command-evidence records initial targeted validations at exit 0 with full deterministic ladder correctly deferred to after review. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are enforced in code: packages/hrx and the validate script are pure deepFrozen descriptor generators and read-only validators with no writeFile/network/child_process/exec, and per-row plus top-level attestations (descriptor_only=true; runtime_execution/payroll/HR-AI-judgment/permission-write/audit-write/product-state-write/runtime-receipt/real-employee-candidate-payroll-data/credential = false) are present; no PII or secrets were found. Authority boundary holds: Claude remains a read-only reviewer (claude_packet.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human final approval is required for runtime opening (deferred to CP00-898), local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/evidence descriptors (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1432-byte stdout, valid JSON), and the request is claude-opus-4-8/max/read-only with Read,Grep,Glob only. One non-blocking P3 provenance nit: the contract's generated_by names a validate-only script while the actual projection generator is unwired. No P0/P1/P2 findings. blocks_goal_closeout is false per scope (pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust); final human/enterprise-trust approval remains outside this receipt.

findings.title: Contract generated_by names a validate-only script; projection generator is unwired
findings.details: The committed contract sets generated_by="scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs", but that script only resolves the contract path and readJson()s it for validation (scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs:16-18); it performs no filesystem write. The canonical projection generator createHrxPeopleContractProjection() (packages/hrx/src/registry.js:342) that the JSON mirrors is not imported by any script that emits the file, so contracts/hrx-people-contract.json is effectively a hand-maintained static artifact with a cosmetic provenance label. This is purely a documentation/maintainability inaccuracy: it does not violate the descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, or no-runtime boundaries, and the file still validates cleanly against the validators.
findings.recommendation: Either add a generator script that emits the contract from createHrxPeopleContractProjection() and point generated_by at it, or change generated_by to indicate the file is hand-maintained and validated by scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking P3; no effect on descriptor-only boundary, no-write/no-real-data posture, or production-ready gating. Safe to proceed to closeout once the full deterministic validation ladder passes.
